old_claim.note: Printed Map Atlases provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the scoped node is about private, large-scale, usually manuscript estate maps; printed atlas publication is a different cartographic product and distribution form.

source_shape.source_locator: History of Cartography chapter 28 says estate maps were large-scale and usually manuscript, resulting from surveying and measuring.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds estate maps in manuscript land-survey practice rather than in printed atlas production.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Printed atlases may share cartographic conventions, but the source-backed estate-map mechanism is local surveying and manuscript plan making, not bound printed map publication.

ontology_before: Estate Survey Maps depended directly on Printed Map Atlases, implying printed atlas production enabled local estate surveying maps.
ontology_after: Estate Survey Maps are scoped to private manuscript land-management maps and do not require printed atlas production as a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: estate_survey_maps no longer directly depends on printed_map_atlases.
would_reject_if: The node were later rescoped to printed estate atlases rather than manuscript estate survey maps.
A stronger source explicitly showed printed atlases were required for early estate-map production.
The printed_map_atlases node were broadened away from bound printed collections into all cartographic drawing.
short_reason: Estate maps were usually manuscript land-survey products, not printed atlases.
